title: CITATION.cff emitter from canonical schema
context: Model cards that are committed to a GitHub repository benefit from a CITATION.cff file so that the card itself is citable as a software artifact, improving the reusability and citability of the documented model. A CITATION.cff emitter would project from the canonical schema's provenance and authors fields into the Citation File Format, making cards more findable and attributable.
objective: Build and ship a CITATION.cff emitter that projects canonical model-card objects into Citation File Format (CFF) v1.2 YAML, enabling model cards committed to GitHub repos to carry a machine-readable citation.
acceptanceCriteria: Emitter projects canonical model-card JSON into valid CITATION.cff YAML (Citation File Format v1.2); output includes cff-version, message, title, authors, date-released, and url fields mapped from the canonical schema.
Output is validated against the CFF v1.2 schema.
Committed golden canonical-to-CFF fixture is diffed in CI.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no credentials or patient data in any committed file.
resources: Citation File Format (CFF) v1.2 spec; GitHub CITATION.cff support documentation
output: A merged PR adding a CITATION.cff emitter with a golden fixture and CI integration.
